## [00:00 - 03:03]

Hi, this is Don Crowther with a video about adding testimonials to your webinar registration page. So this is one of those counterintuitive elements of registration pages that I referred to earlier. It seems like testimonials should increase registrations, but most of the time they actually decrease them. Again, we have a testing issue here because the quality of the testimonials and what the testimonials are about, whether it's about this specific webinar, about you, or about your products, and how your testimonials are actually worded and portrayed. Are they a video? Are they wording? Do you put the person's name? Do you put their location? Do you put a picture of the person? All of those elements have a lot to do with your test. And so for me to just blankly say, thou shalt not do testimonials on webinar, on webinar registration pages, is actually would be a disservice to you. But here's what we find. Most of the tests that we run prove to be negative. Now, I hate trying to explain why tests results the way that they do, because you generate assumptions that may or may not be accurate. But I do have a hypothesis here, which is the testimonials on the registration page may take the focus off of signing up for the webinar, cause people to start thinking more about the process, and they get confused. And a confused mind doesn't buy or in this case, sign up for a webinar. So here's the way I invite you to look at it. If you have some amazing testimonials, you have a webinar that you're going that you're exposing a lot of people to, like tens of thousands of people to, so that you've got enough traffic that you can actually test it, then go ahead and test them. But if you don't have that situation, I'd only use them if I really had killer testimonials specifically talking about this particular webinar. So if you use them, keep them short, show a picture of the testimonial, give her include their name, and resist the notion that just because they're in your page builder template that you should include them, just because they're there doesn't mean they're a good idea. Just delete that section. Now all that said, I don't want you to get the idea that testimonials are a bad thing associated with webinars. Exactly the opposite is true. We are absolutely going to use them in emails and ads, encouraging people to register for the webinar, attend the webinar, watch the replay. They're incredibly valuable. They just generally don't increase signups when shown on the webinar

## [03:03 - 03:30]

registration page. So we still want to go get them. So we want to have a system to collect them. And I actually show you this in a separate video in a separate module, I show you a very slick way to collect testimonials about your webinar, and then be able to use them in the emails and the other things that you're doing to promote your webinar. It's just that we're not going to put them on the registration page itself.
